---
title: "Configuration"
description: "ipmideck reads data/config.yaml and accepts IPMILINK_-prefixed environment overrides."
---

## config.yaml

ipmideck reads its settings from `data/config.yaml`, written with sensible defaults on the
first run. You can edit the file directly, or override any value at runtime with an
environment variable (handy for Docker — see below).

```yaml
# data/config.yaml — auto-generated on first run
server:
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  port: 3000
  https: false
auth:
  enabled: true
  session_expiry: "24h"
  max_login_attempts: 5
ipmi:
  poll_interval: 30
  power_poll_interval: 30
  command_timeout: 30
data:
  retention_days: 365
  cleanup_interval: "24h"
logging:
  level: "info"
modules:
  sensors:
    enabled: true
  fanpilot:
    enabled: true
  power:
    enabled: true
  sel:
    enabled: true
  fru:
    enabled: true
```

The keys above are the real, unprefixed nested keys ipmideck writes to `config.yaml`.
Environment-variable overrides use a separate `IPMILINK_` prefix and never appear inside
the YAML file itself.

## Environment overrides

Every setting can be overridden at runtime with an `IPMILINK_`-prefixed environment
variable. This is the easiest way to configure ipmideck in Docker, where you do not want
to edit a file inside the container.

| Environment variable | Description |
|----------------------|-------------|
| `IPMILINK_SERVER_PORT` | HTTP port the dashboard binds to (default 3000). |
| `IPMILINK_AUTH_ENABLED` | Enable or disable the built-in login (default true). |
| `IPMILINK_IPMI_POLL_INTERVAL` | Seconds between BMC sensor polls (default 30). |
| `IPMILINK_DATA_RETENTION_DAYS` | Days of sensor history to keep before cleanup (default 365). |

<Note>
  Environment variables take precedence over values in `config.yaml`. Set them in your
  `docker run` command (`-e IPMILINK_SERVER_PORT=8080`) or your `docker-compose.yml`.
</Note>

## Where config lives

- **Docker:** `/data/config.yaml` (persist `/data` with a volume).
- **pip:** `~/.ipmideck/config.yaml`.
